title: Ground step — structured_output (M1 SHAPE) for the Evidence Boundary step
description: Ground converts a Triaged intent into bounded·sourced·current facts + explicit uncertainty. It does NOT interpret impact, decide actions, judge risk, or change code (Boundary). TARGET RUNTIME: grammar-constrained generation (M1 = SHAPE — the model cannot emit a wrong shape/enum/missing-required field at token generation). This schema enforces SHAPE ONLY. Truth-bearing fields (CountClaim value↔raw_stdout, racing_changes vs head_start≠head_end, ED AMBIGUOUS/INFERRED edges all mapped to unknowns/conflicts, reviewer rule entry_nodes≥1 OR referent_unresolved, provenance source_tool actually produced the value) are closed by SIBLING deterministic code checks listed in x-validator-contracts below — NOT by any keyword here. §5: input_refs are Postgres RowRefs, never file paths (legacy '.blazewrit/grounds/**' / 'flow-history/<id>.json' are PRE-§5).

allOf.$comment: P1 escalate relaxation: on ground_result.status=escalate Ground does NOT produce the normal Output (정상 [Output] 미산출 또는 부분) — task_subgraph is emitted as its Omitted branch (R22, never truly absent under additionalProperties:false) and volatile_state/freshness are relaxed from the top-level required set. On ok/degraded_pass the full measured output is required.

then.$comment: escalate: keep task_subgraph required (its Omitted branch represents the omission), but drop volatile_state/freshness from required — they may be absent or partial.

allOf.$comment: P0 override (prose [Adaptive Depth]): flow_type=bugfix-p0 forces depth=shallow regardless of any deepen trigger. This is a cross-field one-value restriction grammar CAN express, so it is closed in M1 here (not left to M2).

freshness.git_head_start/git_head_end are conditionally required ONLY when ground_result.status!=escalate AND volatile_state.git.git_state=repo (prose [git capture states] + escalate-survival rule): on escalate, volatile_state/freshness are dropped from required even if git was captured before the ED-down escalate, so this branch must NOT fire on escalate (otherwise it would re-require freshness.git_head_start that the escalate `then` deliberately relaxed). When git_state ∈ {no_repo,empty_repo,git_unavailable} head_start/head_end are omitted + omitted_fields recorded and racing_changes=false.

ground_result.$comment: P1 — Ground's single most-emphasized design element: the discriminated success+failure result enum, reusing Investigate's compatibility_verdict.result shape. status=ok|degraded_pass|escalate. degrade_reasons required-non-empty when degraded_pass; escalate_detail required when escalate (failure_origin restricted to the shared FailureOrigin values Ground may attribute: ground | cap_exceeded). The reviewer gates directly on ground_result.status. This closes the 'success branch undefined' hole.
ground_result.description: Ground's top-level discriminated result (P1 — success branch declared alongside degrade/escalate in one enum). status=ok (all primary activities normal), degraded_pass (ENHANCEMENT-grade losses only — conditional volatile absent/timeout, racing residue, git_state≠repo, unrecognized flow_type fallback), escalate (PRIMARY loss / mechanical error — ED MCP absent/error/timeout, input-precondition violation, malformed active_flow_state → failure_origin=ground; OR step budget exhausted → failure_origin=cap_exceeded). x-validator-contract (M2): degrade_reasons non-empty when degraded_pass; escalate_detail present when escalate.

degrade_reasons.$comment: Required-non-empty when status=degraded_pass (reviewer: 'degraded_pass면 degrade_reasons 비어있지 않음'). Each reason names the ENHANCEMENT-grade loss that demoted the run.
degrade_reasons.description: Why the run is degraded (conditional volatile absent/timeout, racing residue, git_state≠repo, unrecognized flow_type fallback). REQUIRED and non-empty when status=degraded_pass.

escalate_detail.$comment: Required when status=escalate. failure_origin restricted to the two origins Ground attributes: 'ground' (Ground cannot do its job — ED absent/precondition/malformed) and 'cap_exceeded' (step budget exhausted — orchestrator-level halt, NO auto-reinvoke). Both are members of the shared FailureOrigin enum.
escalate_detail.description: Escalate carrier (required when status=escalate). failure_origin ∈ {ground, cap_exceeded}; reason states what failed; evidence references the input ref / tool_call that proves it.

failure_origin.description: Which origin Ground attributes the escalate to. Restricted to {ground, cap_exceeded} (shared FailureOrigin enum).

evidence.description: Single ref string proving the escalate: an input ref or tool_call ref (e.g. attempted ed_query ref on ED-down). On cap_exceeded it is a SINGLE ref summarizing the partial progress (a progress-checkpoint ref to the completed/incomplete activities) — NOT a structured activity list, since this field is one string.

omitted_fields.$comment: R22 carrier. Every PROFILE-DECLARED conditional/degradable schema key that Ground could not capture is recorded here with {field, reason, source_tool} — the mechanism every key-omission/degrade depends on (reviewer: 'profile이 선언한 schema key가 omit될 때마다 omitted_fields에 1행'). A conditional field ABSENT because the flow_type's profile does not declare it (normal profile absence — e.g. perf_baseline on a feature flow) needs NO row. Optional at the grammar level (present only when something declared was omitted); the M2 reviewer enforces non-empty-when-any-declared-key-omitted and forbids value:null / comment-only key markers.
omitted_fields.description: R22 key-omission carrier. One row per PROFILE-DECLARED schema key that tool/data absence forced Ground to omit (e.g. perf_baseline capture failed WITHIN a Performance flow, git head_* on git_state≠repo, ed_snapshot_version when task_subgraph Omitted, unrecognized_flow conditional dimension). A conditional field absent because the flow_type profile does not declare it is normal profile absence and is NOT recorded here. x-validator-contract (M2): every omitted profile-declared conditional key has exactly one row here; no value:null / '# OMITTED' placeholders (R22).

field.description: Schema field path that was omitted (e.g. 'perf_baseline', 'volatile_state.git.head_start', 'freshness.ed_snapshot_version').

source_tool.$comment: Provenance for the omission. For git-omission rows (git_state≠repo dropping branch/dirty/head_*/recent_commits) this is the git-state DETECTION tool (e.g. 'git rev-parse') — the SAME value as the VolatileGit non-repo branch source_tool (no provenance fork). For an ed_snapshot_version omission row it is the ED query tool. A git-omission is a factual non-measurement (no command produced a value to omit), so its provenance is the detection that established git_state.
source_tool.description: Tool/origin that establishes why this field was omitted. git-omission rows carry the git-state detection tool (e.g. 'git rev-parse', identical to VolatileGit non-repo source_tool); ed_snapshot_version omission carries the ED query tool.

flow_id.$comment: §5: the owning flow row id (Postgres flows.id), not a file path.
flow_id.description: The flow this Ground run belongs to. §5 RowRef (Postgres flows.id) — legacy prose 'flow_id: <id>' is a row ref, never a file.

captured_at.description: When this Ground output was produced (prose 'captured_at: ISO8601').

input_refs.$comment: §5: legacy 'input_refs: { triage_output, request_text, conversation_context, scope_hint }' — triage_output becomes a RowRef; the rest are inline literals (raw input, not cross-step rows).
input_refs.description: References to the inputs this Ground run consumed. §5: triage_ref is a RowRef (Postgres step_runs row id of the Triage output) — NOT a file path. request_text / conversation_context_digest / scope_hint are inline literals (the raw caller input, not cross-step rows).

triage_ref.description: RowRef to the Triage step_runs row that produced flow_type/classification_metadata/clarifications.

request_text.description: Original caller request text (inline literal, not a row ref).

conversation_context_digest.$comment: Channel↔digest coupling is a cross-field TRUTH constraint spanning two top-level properties (channel + input_refs.conversation_context_digest), so it is an x-validator-contract (M2), NOT grammar-enforced here (a sibling-key 'iff' is not expressible at this nesting level without re-litigating envelope shape).
conversation_context_digest.description: Digest of None-state conversation turns. Non-empty (minLength:1) — even the zero-turn user_session case carries a real digest OVER empty input, never the empty string (prose '빈 문자열 아님'). x-validator-contract (M2): conversation_context_digest is present IFF channel=user_session (a2a/ci have none — must be ABSENT, not empty-string). A user_session turn with ZERO conversation_context turns STILL carries this key (a digest OVER empty input — not an empty string, not absent); key absence is reserved for a2a/ci only. So the IFF fires on channel alone, never on turn-count. Inline literal.

scope_hint.description: Monorepo package/path scope limiter extracted by Triage or supplied by caller (optional). Inline literal.

channel.description: Input channel. Ground is channel-agnostic; conversation_context_digest exists only for user_session.

flow_type.description: Triage-classified flow type. Drives which conditional volatile_state.* fields are present (Volatile Profile by Flow Type) and the deepen triggers. Free string because the flow set is the M4 flow-definition source, not this schema's concern; the orchestrator validates it against the flow definitions.

depth.$comment: Adaptive Step Depth Policy. P0 override: bugfix-p0 forces shallow regardless of deepen triggers — enforced in M1 by the top-level allOf (flow_type=bugfix-p0 → depth const shallow).
depth.description: Adaptive depth this Ground run executed at (shallow default; deep on a deepen trigger). P0 override (flow_type=bugfix-p0) forces shallow (grammar-enforced via the top-level conditional).

active_flow_note.$comment: Active Flow Conflict priority. active:<something> on entry is a MECHANICAL ERROR (orchestrator bug) — Ground escalates; that escalation is an orchestrator-level outcome, not a value Ground self-reports as a normal field. Here Ground records only the post-resolution residue it was ASKED to be aware of (suspended-similar hint, preempted prior for post-stabilization follow-up). The input↔note linkage cannot be grammar-enforced (active_flow_state is a runtime INPUT, not in this output), so it is an x-validator-contract (M2): see the property $comments and prose [active_flow_state].
active_flow_note.description: Awareness of post-resolution active-flow residue (Ground does NOT resolve conflicts — orchestrator resolves BEFORE Ground entry). Optional. Records a suspended-similar-area hint and/or a preempted prior id for post-stabilization follow-up. NOTE: active:<something> on entry = mechanical error → Ground escalates (an orchestrator outcome), not represented here. x-validator-contract (M2): preempted_prior_ref is present IFF input active_flow_state.preempted!=null; suspended_similar_hint is present IFF the similarity check found a path-prefix overlap (and then at least one matching unknowns[{dim:suspended_similar}] item must also exist — one per overlapping prior, so possibly many; the hint mirrors the suspended_similar dimension COLLECTIVELY, not 1:1). The unknowns items are the authoritative carrier; the hint is the advisory mirror.

suspended_similar_hint.$comment: Advisory mirror of the authoritative unknowns[{dim:suspended_similar}] carrier. x-validator-contract (M2): present IFF the similarity check fired; when present, at least one matching unknowns item must exist (one per overlapping prior — possibly many; the single-string hint mirrors the dimension collectively, not 1:1).
suspended_similar_hint.description: Free-text hint mirroring the unknowns[{dim:suspended_similar}] item surfaced when a suspended prior is in a similar area (similarity check). The unknowns item is authoritative; this hint is advisory. Boundary: a hint, not a judgment.

preempted_prior_ref.$comment: x-validator-contract (M2): present IFF input active_flow_state.preempted!=null (the P0-preempt-direct case in prose [active_flow_state]).
preempted_prior_ref.description: RowRef of a prior flow preempted by a P0 (recorded for post-stabilization follow-up). x-validator-contract (M2): present IFF input active_flow_state.preempted!=null. §5 RowRef, not a file.

task_subgraph.$comment: M3 DEGRADE: ED MCP can be ABSENT/fail/timeout → the whole subgraph is an Omitted branch (which co-occurs with ground_result.status=escalate, since ED is PRIMARY). R18: god_nodes_in_scope is omitted when ED is absent. Omitted.reason mapping by escalate origin: failure_origin=ground (ED-down, prose ED table) → MCP not attached='unavailable', ED errored='tool_failed', ED timed out='timeout'; failure_origin=cap_exceeded (step budget exhausted before the ED query completed / was ever attempted) → 'skipped' (the subgraph was skipped for budget, not tool-failed). The shared Omitted enum has no escalate/primary_down member — escalate-grade is carried by ground_result.status, not by Omitted.reason. The Omitted branch below restricts reason to EXACTLY {unavailable, tool_failed, timeout, skipped} (tool_absent/not_applicable are forbidden for task_subgraph — there is no non-escalate Omitted subgraph state). The reason↔failure_origin pairing (ground→three-down-reasons, cap_exceeded→skipped) is an x-validator-contract (M2); grammar only narrows the enum. Specializes Measured.value with the concrete bounded-subgraph shape; reuses shared Omitted. Reviewer rule (M2): entry_nodes≥1 OR unknowns contains a referent_unresolved item.
task_subgraph.description: M3 DEGRADE union. The bounded ED subgraph (entry nodes + neighbors + god nodes in scope). ED MCP is degradable: when ED is ABSENT/fails/times out the model MUST emit the Omitted branch (R22 — not null, not a placeholder) and surface the gap in unknowns/conflicts; R18 god_nodes_in_scope only exist in the measured branch. Grammar forces exactly one branch via the 'status' discriminant. x-validator-contract (M2): reviewer rule — entry_nodes length ≥ 1 OR unknowns contains an item with dim='referent' reason='unresolved' (referent_unresolved). Also: every ED AMBIGUOUS/INFERRED edge is mapped into unknowns or conflicts (silent-gap forbidden).

oneOf.$comment: additionalProperties:false at the branch's OWN object level (consistent with every other closed object in this schema) — a fabricated stray top-level key inside task_subgraph is rejected. The allowed keys are exactly the Measured envelope's: status, value, source_tool, plus the optional source provenance block.

entry_nodes.description: Entry nodes resolved from request_text + clarifications + scope_hint. Reviewer rule (M2): length ≥ 1 OR a referent_unresolved unknown exists. Each node carries source + freshness (provenance floor).

neighbors.description: Neighbor nodes within the bounded expansion of the entry nodes.

god_nodes_in_scope.$comment: R18: present only in the measured branch (ED available). When ED is absent the whole subgraph is Omitted, so this field cannot exist (grammar-enforced).
god_nodes_in_scope.description: High-degree 'god nodes' inside the scoped subgraph (capped by god-node expansion limit). Exists only when ED is available (measured branch). R18: omit when ED absent — realized structurally by the Omitted branch having no value.

bounded_at.$comment: R23: a token-budget cutoff measured from the ED query — modeled as CountClaim, not a bare integer.
bounded_at.description: The token count at which the subgraph expansion was bounded (token-budget / god-node cap). R23 CountClaim — a measured count, never a bare integer; source.command is the ED query whose token tally produced it.

ed_snapshot_version.description: ED graph snapshot version/hash the subgraph was read at (mirrored into freshness.ed_snapshot_version).

oneOf.$comment: task_subgraph Omitted co-occurs ONLY with ground_result.status=escalate (ED is PRIMARY; there is no non-escalate Omitted subgraph state — degenerate/referent-absent ED cases are MEASURED). The Omitted.reason is restricted to {unavailable, tool_failed, timeout, skipped}: the first three are the failure_origin=ground ED-down mapping (prose [ED MCP] table: MCP not attached→unavailable, ED errored→tool_failed, ED timed out→timeout); 'skipped' is the failure_origin=cap_exceeded case (step budget exhausted before the ED query completed / was ever attempted — the subgraph was skipped, not tool-failed). The remaining shared Omitted members (tool_absent/not_applicable) are NOT legal for task_subgraph. additionalProperties:false is restated at this branch's OWN object level (consistent with the measured branch and every other closed object in this schema) so a fabricated stray key is rejected by THIS allOf member too, not only by the shared Omitted def. x-validator-contract (M2): {status≠escalate AND task_subgraph.status=omitted} is a reviewer FAIL ('ED absence must escalate'); reason=skipped is valid ONLY when failure_origin=cap_exceeded, and {unavailable,tool_failed,timeout} ONLY when failure_origin=ground.

volatile_state.$comment: Each tool field is a DegradableMeasurement (M3). Universal: typecheck/tests/lint/git. Conditional (present-or-absent by flow_type): perf_baseline/dependency_audit/observability/release_state. 'skipped-with-reason' in prose = Omitted(reason:skipped). git is non-degradable factual state (always present). tests.passed + tests.failed-count + lint.warnings/errors + release_state.new_commits_count = CountClaim (R23). NOTE: the shared Omitted enum includes 'not_applicable', but GROUND NEVER EMITS not_applicable — every Ground measurement omission maps to {skipped, tool_absent, unavailable, timeout, tool_failed} (command-not-defined=skipped; profile-undeclared conditional fields are absent keys, not Omitted branches). This single-enum-member exclusion is grammar-expressible, so it is M1-CLOSED: every Ground volatile Omitted arm (typecheck/tests/lint + the four conditionals) $refs the shared Omitted wrapped in an allOf that narrows reason to {tool_absent, tool_failed, timeout, unavailable, skipped} — not_applicable is structurally unproducible (symmetric with the task_subgraph Omitted reason restriction, no asymmetry). CONDITIONAL-FIELD GATE (M2): the four conditional keys (perf_baseline/dependency_audit/observability/release_state) are optional here at the GRAMMAR level because flow_type is a free string (the M4 flow-definition source, not this schema's concern), so grammar cannot gate presence on flow_type. A conditional key may be PRESENT only if the current flow_type's profile (prose [Volatile Profile by Flow Type]) DECLARES it (perf_baseline⇒performance, dependency_audit⇒migration, observability⇒bugfix-unreproducible, release_state⇒release); its presence in any non-declaring flow_type (e.g. perf_baseline on a chore/feature flow) is a reviewer FAIL ('conditional volatile field present in a flow whose profile does not declare it'). This shuts the hole where a fabricated conditional measurement passes both grammar and reviewer.
volatile_state.description: Volatile capture per the declared profile for this flow_type. Universal fields (typecheck, tests, lint, git) are ALWAYS keys; tool-dependent ones are M3 DegradableMeasurement (measured | omitted-with-reason — prose 'skipped-with-reason' = Omitted(reason='skipped'); 'timeout'/'fail' map to Omitted(reason='timeout'|'tool_failed') or a measured-with-failure status per field). Conditional fields (perf_baseline / dependency_audit / observability / release_state) are PRESENT-OR-ABSENT by flow_type — Ground does NOT judge which measurement matters; the flow definition declares the profile. x-validator-contract (M2): each profile-required command has an explicit status; every capture failure/timeout is mapped into unknowns/conflicts.

typecheck.description: M3 DegradableMeasurement. Measured branch carries an explicit pass/fail status + provenance; Omitted carries reason (skipped-with-reason / timeout / tool_failed).

tests.description: M3 DegradableMeasurement. Measured value carries pass/fail status, passed (CountClaim), failed-list, failed_count (CountClaim), optional coverage. Omitted when no test command (skipped) / timeout / fail-to-run.

lint.description: M3 DegradableMeasurement. Measured value carries warnings (CountClaim) + errors (CountClaim). Omitted when no lint command / timeout / fail.

git.$comment: git is a FACTUAL-STATE discriminant ([git capture states]), NOT 'always a repo'. git_state ∈ {repo, no_repo, empty_repo, git_unavailable}. Only git_state=repo carries branch/dirty/head_start/head_end/recent_commits; the other states omit those keys (+ omitted_fields row) and demote the run to degraded_pass. The reviewer gates 'git_state 부재 = FAIL'.
git.description: Git capture state. Discriminated on git_state: repo (git repo with ≥1 commit) carries branch/dirty/head_start/head_end/recent_commits + source_tool; no_repo (working dir is not a git repo) / empty_repo (repo with 0 commits, no HEAD) / git_unavailable (git binary absent/unrunnable) carry only git_state + source_tool, omit the branch/dirty/head_* / recent_commits keys (recorded in omitted_fields), and the run becomes degraded_pass. Not a fake 'always present' measurement — non-repo/empty/no-binary are real factual states surfaced (not escalate, since git is not a PRIMARY tool).

perf_baseline.$comment: Conditional (Performance flow). Present-or-absent by flow_type. When present, still M3 (the perf command can fail/timeout).
perf_baseline.description: CONDITIONAL (Performance flow). M3 DegradableMeasurement. Measured value = { p50, p95, p99, throughput, command }. Absent for non-Performance flows; Omitted when the perf command fails/times out within a Performance flow.

dependency_audit.description: CONDITIONAL (Migration flow). M3 DegradableMeasurement. Measured value = { packages:[{name,current,latest,breaking}], lockfile_hash }. Absent for non-Migration flows.

observability.description: CONDITIONAL (Bug Fix Unreproducible flow). M3 DegradableMeasurement. Measured value = { logs_query, metrics_query, traces_query, results }. Absent for other flows; Omitted when the observability backend is unavailable.

release_state.$comment: Conditional (Release flow). new_commits_count is a measured count → CountClaim (R23).
release_state.description: CONDITIONAL (Release flow). M3 DegradableMeasurement. Measured value = { last_version, new_commits_count (CountClaim), changelog_entries }. Absent for non-Release flows.

unknowns.$comment: Provenance floor: every item REQUIRES source_tool. R18/silent-gap: ED AMBIGUOUS/INFERRED edges + capture failures map here. referent_unresolved is the reviewer-rule escape hatch (dim='referent', reason='unresolved').
unknowns.description: Explicit unknowns surfaced (silent gaps forbidden — every model/uncertainty stated). Each item carries dim, reason, source_tool (provenance floor, REQUIRED), attempted_at. ED AMBIGUOUS/INFERRED edges, capture failures/timeouts, and an unresolved request referent (dim='referent', reason='unresolved' — the referent_unresolved case) all land here. x-validator-contract (M2): every ED AMBIGUOUS/INFERRED edge and every capture failure is present here or in conflicts.

conflicts.$comment: Provenance floor: source_tool REQUIRED. Boundary (R18 semantic residual): 'description' must STATE the conflict, not interpret/compare/derive — that residual is not grammar-closable (irreducible floor).
conflicts.description: Surfaced conflicts between sources (tool|file outputs that disagree). Each item carries sources[], description, source_tool (provenance floor, REQUIRED). Boundary: description STATES the conflict; it must not interpret, rank, or derive (R18 semantic-boundary residual — irreducible floor, mitigated not eliminated; no grammar keyword can forbid an interpretive sentence inside a string).

freshness.$comment: racing_changes is a bool the model SETS; the M2 validator asserts it == (git_head_start != git_head_end after the 1 retry). head_start/head_end mirror volatile_state.git and exist ONLY when git_state=repo — so they are NOT in the base required set; the top-level conditional allOf (git_state=repo → require git_head_start/git_head_end) is the sole source of their requiredness. On git_state ∈ {no_repo,empty_repo,git_unavailable} they are omitted (+ omitted_fields row) and racing_changes=false. racing_changes is ALWAYS present.
freshness.description: Freshness/racing-changes metadata. racing_changes is ALWAYS present; git_head_start/git_head_end are present (and required) ONLY when volatile_state.git.git_state=repo — when git_state≠repo they are omitted (+ omitted_fields row) and racing_changes=false. ed_snapshot_version is reviewer-required when ED was measured. racing_changes is set true when head_start ≠ head_end after the single capture retry. x-validator-contract (M2): racing_changes == (git_head_start != git_head_end) when git_state=repo, else false; ed_snapshot_version matches task_subgraph.value.ed_snapshot_version when the subgraph is measured.

ed_snapshot_version.$comment: Reviewer-required factual provenance. Optional at the GRAMMAR level because on ground_result.status=escalate the entire freshness block is relaxed from required (the escalate `then` drops freshness), and ED-Omitted ALWAYS co-occurs with escalate (ED is PRIMARY → escalate, never a non-escalate ED-Omitted state). On ok/degraded_pass task_subgraph is always MEASURED, so this is M2-required there (mirrors task_subgraph.value.ed_snapshot_version). It is NOT left optional to model a non-escalate ED-omitted state — that state cannot exist under the contract.
ed_snapshot_version.description: ED snapshot version/hash recorded for freshness. Optional at grammar level because on escalate the whole freshness block is relaxed; on ok/degraded_pass it is M2-required since task_subgraph is always measured there (and must equal task_subgraph.value.ed_snapshot_version).

git_head_start.description: git HEAD at capture START (reviewer-required). Compared to git_head_end to detect racing changes.

racing_changes.$comment: Set true only if head_start ≠ head_end AFTER the single retry (prose: 'capture 시작·종료 git HEAD 비교 → 변동 시 racing_changes 1회 재시도', reviewer: '재시도 1회 후도 변동 시 표시'). On git_state≠repo, or when git_state TRANSITIONED within the capture window (start repo → end non-repo/HEAD-gone — prose [racing_changes] capture-window transition), the comparison is skipped/unstable → racing_changes=false and the instability is surfaced via unknowns (dim racing_changes|capture_failure) + degraded_pass, NOT left undefined.
racing_changes.description: true if git HEAD changed between capture start and end and was still different after the one retry. x-validator-contract (M2): racing_changes == (git_head_start != git_head_end) when git_state stayed=repo across the window; if git_state was unstable/transitioned within the window the comparison is skipped (racing_changes=false) and the instability is surfaced in unknowns + degraded_pass.

verification_proof.$comment: Shared container. Hashes are SHAPE; re-hash + replay + re-read are M2.
verification_proof.description: Provenance container backing every fact (tool_calls, read_files, optional ed_queries). SHAPE here; x-validator-contract (M2): re-hash every *_sha256/result_hash, re-read read_files, replay replayable tool_calls. Prose 'verification_proof 해시만으론 부족' — that is exactly why per-item source_tool is ALSO required on unknowns/conflicts/nodes (provenance floor). ESCALATE PROOF-OF-ATTEMPT (M2, NOT grammar): the shared VerificationProof puts NO minItems on tool_calls/ed_queries (an empty array is grammar-legal), so grammar cannot force a non-empty proof. When ground_result.status=escalate with failure_origin=ground (ED-down in particular), the reviewer asserts verification_proof contains AT LEAST ONE attempted tool_call/ed_query proving the escalate (an attempted ed_query for ED-down) and that escalate_detail.evidence references it — the 'attempted tool_call 포함' guarantee in the escalate field-survival rule is an M2-only invariant, not a grammar one. cap_exceeded escalate (budget exhausted before measurement) is exempt.

source_manifest.description: R36. Machine-readable manifest of every file Ground cited (path+sha256+line_count+cited_lines). Ground is the canonical fact-citation step (reads CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/.claude/rules). x-m2-validator-contract: every file:line reference emitted anywhere in this output MUST exist in source_manifest.cited_lines, and each sha256 must re-hash equal at Verify (M2 code check; grammar enforces only the SHAPE).

SubgraphNode.$comment: Provenance floor: every fact node carries source + freshness. source is a SourceTool provenance field (not a fixed const) — subgraph nodes are produced by the ED query, but source records that provenance rather than constraining it to a literal; freshness is per-node last_updated.
SubgraphNode.description: A node in the bounded ED subgraph (entry / neighbor / god node). Provenance floor: id + source (SourceTool provenance — the ED query origin for subgraph nodes) + freshness (per-node last_updated, prose 'per-node: freshness metadata (last_updated, source)').

id.description: Stable node identifier within the ED graph.

source.$comment: Provenance floor (source_tool). Subgraph nodes are produced by the ED query; source records that provenance (SourceTool), it is not a fixed const.
source.description: Provenance: how this node was obtained. SourceTool — for subgraph nodes this is the ED query origin (provenance field, not a fixed literal).

VolatileCheck.$comment: typecheck — M3 union. Measured value carries a pass/fail STATUS (run succeeded but result may be fail) + provenance hash; Omitted = not-run/timeout/tool-absent.
VolatileCheck.description: M3 DegradableMeasurement for typecheck. Measured = the command RAN (status success|fail of the type-check itself) with output_hash + source command provenance. Omitted = command not defined (skipped-with-reason) / timeout / tool_failed. NOTE: a failing type-check is a MEASURED branch (it ran and reported fail); only inability-to-run is Omitted.

result.description: Outcome of the type-check run itself (the command executed). 'success' / 'fail'. Boundary: a raw outcome, not an interpretation.

oneOf.$comment: Ground-emit Omitted: reason restricted to the 5 Ground reasons {tool_absent, tool_failed, timeout, unavailable, skipped} — not_applicable is EXCLUDED in-grammar (Ground never emits it; same single-enum-member restriction grammar-closed for task_subgraph). Closes the orphan-enum-member symmetrically rather than leaving it M2-only.

VolatileTests.$comment: tests — M3 union. passed + failed_count are CountClaim (R23). failed is the list of failing test ids. coverage optional.
VolatileTests.description: M3 DegradableMeasurement for tests. Measured value carries result (success|fail of the suite run), passed (CountClaim — R23), failed_count (CountClaim — R23, length of failed list), failed (list of failing test identifiers, each with source_tool), optional coverage, source_command, output_hash, captured_at, duration_ms. Omitted when no test command (skipped) / timeout / suite failed to run.

result.description: Suite-run outcome (the command executed). 'success' = all passed, 'fail' = at least one failed. Raw outcome, not a judgment.

passed.$comment: R23 CountClaim — measured count, never a bare integer.
passed.description: Number of passing tests. R23 CountClaim; source.command is the test command, raw_stdout_sha256 backs the count.

failed.description: Identifiers of failing tests. Each item carries source_tool (provenance floor).

failed_count.$comment: R23 CountClaim. M2: equals failed.length AND derivable from raw_stdout.
failed_count.description: Number of failing tests. R23 CountClaim. x-validator-contract (M2): value == failed array length AND derivable from source.raw_stdout.

coverage.description: Optional coverage figure (percentage 0-100). Present only if the test command emits coverage.

oneOf.$comment: Ground-emit Omitted: reason restricted to the 5 Ground reasons {tool_absent, tool_failed, timeout, unavailable, skipped} — not_applicable EXCLUDED in-grammar (Ground never emits it).

VolatileLint.$comment: lint — M3 union. warnings + errors are CountClaim (R23).
VolatileLint.description: M3 DegradableMeasurement for lint. Measured value carries warnings (CountClaim — R23), errors (CountClaim — R23), source_command, output_hash, captured_at, duration_ms. Omitted when no lint command (skipped) / timeout / fail-to-run.

warnings.description: Lint warning count. R23 CountClaim — never a bare integer.

errors.description: Lint error count. R23 CountClaim — never a bare integer.

oneOf.$comment: Ground-emit Omitted: reason restricted to the 5 Ground reasons {tool_absent, tool_failed, timeout, unavailable, skipped} — not_applicable EXCLUDED in-grammar (Ground never emits it).

VolatileGit.$comment: git — FACTUAL-STATE discriminant ([git capture states]), NOT 'always a committed repo'. git_state ∈ {repo, no_repo, empty_repo, git_unavailable}. ONLY git_state=repo requires branch/dirty/head_start/head_end/recent_commits (each commit carries sha + source_tool provenance); the other three states require only git_state + source_tool and the branch/dirty/head_*/recent_commits keys are omitted (recorded in omitted_fields) — the run is then degraded_pass. recent_commits is a list, not a count, so no CountClaim.
VolatileGit.description: Git capture state, discriminated on git_state. git_state=repo (git repo with ≥1 commit): branch, dirty(bool), head_start, head_end, recent_commits (each with sha + provenance source_tool). git_state ∈ {no_repo (working dir not a git repo), empty_repo (repo with 0 commits / no HEAD), git_unavailable (git binary absent/unrunnable)}: branch/dirty/head_*/recent_commits keys are OMITTED (+ omitted_fields row), racing_changes comparison skipped (racing_changes=false), run demoted to degraded_pass. git is a factual state, not a PRIMARY tool, so non-repo/empty/no-binary are surfaced (degrade), never escalate.

recent_commits.description: Recent commits from git log. Each carries sha + provenance. Present only when git_state=repo.

source_tool.$comment: Provenance floor for the git facts as a whole.
source_tool.description: Provenance for the git capture (e.g. 'git status'/'git log' command).

oneOf.$comment: git_state ∈ {no_repo, empty_repo, git_unavailable} — branch/dirty/head_*/recent_commits keys OMITTED (recorded in omitted_fields), run degraded_pass.

git_state.description: Non-repo factual state: no_repo / empty_repo / git_unavailable. head_*/recent_commits omitted.

source_tool.description: Provenance for the git-state detection (e.g. 'git rev-parse' result).

VolatilePerfBaseline.$comment: Conditional (Performance). M3 union. p50/p95/p99/throughput are measured timings, not COUNTS — left as numbers (CountClaim is for counts-of-things, these are latency/throughput measurements). Modeled as Measured with provenance.
VolatilePerfBaseline.description: CONDITIONAL (Performance flow) M3 DegradableMeasurement. Measured value = { p50, p95, p99, throughput, command, captured_at }. Latency/throughput are measurements (numbers with a command + hash provenance), not counts — so NOT CountClaim. Omitted when the perf harness fails/times out.

oneOf.$comment: Ground-emit Omitted: reason restricted to the 5 Ground reasons {tool_absent, tool_failed, timeout, unavailable, skipped} — not_applicable EXCLUDED in-grammar (Ground never emits it).

VolatileDependencyAudit.$comment: Conditional (Migration). M3 union. packages list; breaking is a per-package bool. lockfile_hash provenance.
VolatileDependencyAudit.description: CONDITIONAL (Migration flow) M3 DegradableMeasurement. Measured value = { packages:[{name,current,latest,breaking}], lockfile_hash }. Omitted when the dependency tool is unavailable.

breaking.description: Whether the latest is a breaking-change bump (factual flag from the audit tool, not Ground's judgment).

oneOf.$comment: Ground-emit Omitted: reason restricted to the 5 Ground reasons {tool_absent, tool_failed, timeout, unavailable, skipped} — not_applicable EXCLUDED in-grammar (Ground never emits it).

VolatileObservability.$comment: Conditional (Bug Fix Unreproducible). M3 union. queries + raw results. results kept as opaque strings (no interpretation — boundary).
VolatileObservability.description: CONDITIONAL (Bug Fix Unreproducible flow) M3 DegradableMeasurement. Measured value = { logs_query, metrics_query, traces_query, results }. results are raw captured payloads (no interpretation — Boundary). Omitted when the observability backend is unavailable.

results.description: Raw captured results for the queries (opaque payload — Ground does not interpret).

oneOf.$comment: Ground-emit Omitted: reason restricted to the 5 Ground reasons {tool_absent, tool_failed, timeout, unavailable, skipped} — not_applicable EXCLUDED in-grammar (Ground never emits it).

VolatileReleaseState.$comment: Conditional (Release). M3 union. new_commits_count = CountClaim (R23 — measured count). changelog_entries list.
VolatileReleaseState.description: CONDITIONAL (Release flow) M3 DegradableMeasurement. Measured value = { last_version, new_commits_count (CountClaim — R23), changelog_entries }. Omitted when version/changelog tooling is unavailable.

new_commits_count.$comment: R23 CountClaim — measured count (e.g. 'git rev-list --count <last_tag>..HEAD'), never a bare integer.
new_commits_count.description: Commits since the last release. R23 CountClaim; source.command is the rev-list count, raw_stdout_sha256 backs it.

oneOf.$comment: Ground-emit Omitted: reason restricted to the 5 Ground reasons {tool_absent, tool_failed, timeout, unavailable, skipped} — not_applicable EXCLUDED in-grammar (Ground never emits it).

Unknown.$comment: Provenance floor: source_tool REQUIRED. dim/reason are enumerated where the prose names them (referent/unresolved) but kept open-ish via string for tool/capture-derived dims. The referent_unresolved case is dim='referent' + reason='unresolved'.
Unknown.description: An explicit unknown item. Provenance floor: source_tool REQUIRED. dim names the dimension of the unknown; reason states why. The reviewer-rule escape hatch is dim='referent' with reason='unresolved' (referent_unresolved → the alternative to entry_nodes≥1).

dim.$comment: FACTUAL-tag dimension (P6 — NOT an Investigate disposition token). Known values (the [unknowns.dim FACTUAL tag enum] table): referent, ed_ambiguous, ed_inferred, ed_drift, capture_failure, timeout, inaccessible, racing_changes, suspended_similar, unrecognized_flow. Kept open (type:string, not a closed enum) because new FACTUAL dimensions may be added — prose 'open-ish' — provided each item is self-describing via reason + source_tool. (external_unknown is an Investigate-produced dimension — Investigate attempts WebFetch — NOT emitted by Ground; it is not in Ground's dim set.)
dim.description: Factual dimension of the unknown (open-ish — new factual dimensions may be added; each item must stay self-describing via reason + source_tool). Known values: referent (request entity absent from ED graph — the referent_unresolved case, reason='unresolved'), ed_ambiguous (ED AMBIGUOUS edge), ed_inferred (ED INFERRED low-confidence edge), ed_drift (ED reports card↔code drift one-sided — single-source uncertainty), capture_failure (volatile command failed, non-timeout), timeout (volatile command timed out), inaccessible (permission/access denied), racing_changes (head_start≠head_end after retry), suspended_similar (similar-area suspended prior exists), unrecognized_flow (flow_type matched no profile row — universal-only fallback). NOT an Investigate disposition token (external_unknown is Investigate-produced, not a Ground dim).

reason.description: Why this is unknown (e.g. 'unresolved' for an absent request referent, 'timeout', 'tool_absent', 'ambiguous_edge').

source_tool.description: Provenance floor (REQUIRED): the tool/origin that surfaced this unknown.

Conflict.$comment: Provenance floor: source_tool REQUIRED. sources[] are the disagreeing tool|file origins. description STATES the conflict; the no-interpretation rule (R18 semantic residual) is the irreducible floor — grammar cannot forbid an interpretive sentence in a string.
Conflict.description: An explicit conflict between sources. Provenance floor: source_tool REQUIRED. sources[] lists the disagreeing tool|file origins. description STATES the conflict (Boundary: no comparison/derivation/judgment — irreducible semantic floor, mitigated not eliminated).

sources.description: The disagreeing origins (tool|file). Minimum 2 to be a conflict.

ref.$comment: Provenance-gap rule (R36): a kind:file ref that is an ED-graph artifact (e.g. a drift card↔code pair Ground did NOT itself read) is an ED-INTERNAL ref and needs NO source_manifest entry; only a kind:file ref that Ground actually READ must appear in source_manifest.cited_lines. x-validator-contract (M2): if a kind:file ref names a path Ground read (in verification_proof.read_files), it MUST exist in source_manifest.entries; ED-internal card/code refs are exempt.
ref.description: The origin reference: a tool name (kind:tool), or for kind:file either an ED-INTERNAL artifact ref (e.g. an ED drift card/code node Ground did not read — no source_manifest entry required) or a READ file path Ground actually read (then it MUST appear in source_manifest.cited_lines). A read input, not a §5 cross-step row ref.

description.description: Statement of the conflict. Boundary: states what disagrees; does NOT interpret/rank/derive (R18 residual — irreducible floor).

source_tool.description: Provenance floor (REQUIRED): the tool/origin that surfaced this conflict.
